docs.title: Bonus FFB
docs.text:
Do you own a force-feedback joystick? Have you wondered if it can simulate other gaming peripherals using only FFB effects? It sure can—that's Bonus FFB!

Bonus FFB is free, open-source software that simulates these devices using force-feedback joysticks:

  • H-pattern shifters
  • Sequential shifters (coming soon)
  • Auto-manual (PRNDL) shifters (coming soon)
  • Handbrakes

docs.title: Supported devices
docs.text:
• MOZA AB9 FFB Base

Help us support more devices

Bonus FFB should work with any FFB joystick that supports DirectInput. If you own a VPForce Rhino, FFBeast, or other powerful FFB joystick and would like to test Bonus FFB, please get in touch via Discord or Github .

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
Required depedencies:

  • vJoy v2.1.8 or newer

Optional (but recommended) dependencies:

  • RenCloud's telemetry plugin for ATS/ETS2

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text:
Danger

Using force-feedback devices for anything other than their intended purpose may result in damage or bodily harm. The authors of Bonus FFB accept no liability for any loss or damage including, without limitation, indirect or consequential loss or damage arising out of or in connection with the use of the software. Use Bonus FFB at your own risk.

docs.title: MOZA AB9
docs.text:
Bonus FFB fully supports the MOZA AB9 FFB base

You must set Force Feedback Mode to DirectInput or Integrated FFB

If Force Feedback Mode is incorrect, Bonus FFB will silently fail to send to force feedback commands to the base, resulting a 'dead stick' effect.

As of January 2025, MOZA's Pit House software crashes when an AB9 FFB base is powered on. This usually doesn't matter, just be sure to fully configure your wheel before powering on the AB9 base. It does prevent devices that must be run through Pit House from running correctly—for example, the MOZA Stalks in ATS/ETS2 compatibility mode.

docs.title: Required MOZA Cockpit settings
docs.text:
Under Basic Settings, change these settings:

  • Force Feedback Mode to DirectInput or Integrated FFB
  • Maximum Torque Output to 100%
  • Overall Force Feedback Intensity to 100%
  • Spring to 0
  • Game Force Feedback Gain to 200%–250%

Damper, Inertia, and Friction can be set to personal preference.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
Using FFB devices for anything other than their intended purpose may result in damage or injury

The authors of Bonus FFB accept no liability for any loss or damage including, without limitation, indirect or consequential loss or damage arising out of or in connection with the use of the software. Use Bonus FFB at your own risk.

docs.title: Install vJoy
docs.text:
Install a recent version of vJoy.

Run vJoyConf and set up at least one virtual device with a minimum of 8 buttons:

docs.title: American Truck Simulator/Euro Truck Simulator 2
docs.text:
Install RenCloud's scs-sdk-plugin DLL to the bin\win_x64\plugins folder of your ATS and ETS2 installations. These are the default locations when using Steam:

  • C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\American Truck Simulator\bin\win_x64\plugins\
  • C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Euro Truck Simulator 2\bin\win_x64\plugins\
When installed correctly, ATS/ETS2 will start with a notice that the SDK has been activated.

Unfortunately you will have to press OK for this message each time the game is launched.

docs.title: Configure your FFB joystick
docs.text:
Follow the device settings guide for your specific FFB joystick.

Some FFB joysticks will silently fail to work with Bonus FFB until correctly configured.

docs.title: Install and configure Bonus FFB
docs.text:
Download and run the latest Bonus FFB installer.

Bonus FFB installs several executables, one for each application:

  • H-Shifter
  • Handbrake

Launch your desired application and open the Input/output settings tab. Select your FFB joystick and other devices as required by the application. Select the vJoy device you configured earlier.

If your FFB joystick and other devices are correctly detected and configured, you can start the app by pressing the ▶️ button.

Please read the application's guide for app-specific configuration, options, and features.

docs.title: H-Shifter
docs.text:
This application simulates an H-pattern shifter with gear grinding, locking, and float shifting. Configuring clutch and throttle pedals axes in the Input/output settings tab is required. Float shifting requires game telemetry.

H-Shifter is best suited to casual driving games, such truck, bus, and taxi sims. Depending on the strength of your FFB joystick, it's possible to overcome the locking, grinding, and channel-keeping force effects. Remember to play along and not push through these effects. If you want something stronger and more precise, consider a purpose-built device like the Bash Pro.

docs.title: Game configuration
docs.text: H-Shifter sends vJoy button presses when gears are engaged. Bind the in-game gear slots as you would with a hardware H-pattern shifter, by walking through the gears slot-by-slot in the game control settings. The input device will show up as the vJoy device you selected in H-Shifter's input/output settings.
